name: fengshui-master-source-quality-policy
purpose: Machine-readable source, citation, and claim-quality policy for maintaining FengShui Master without blurring classical vocabulary, lineage methods, modern symbolic adapters, and high-stakes professional boundaries.

source_tiers.label: Classical anchor
acceptable_evidence: careful secondary summary of a classical text
source_tiers.output_posture: Use as classical background or traditional vocabulary, not as proof that one text defines all feng shui practice.
limitations: state when a claim is a broad anchor rather than an operational formula

source_tiers.label: Scholarly or reference summary
source_tiers.output_posture: Use for cautious background, definitions, and school mapping.
limitations: do not treat summaries as lineage authorization
verify current or contested claims before operational use

source_tiers.label: School or lineage practice
source_tiers.output_posture: Name the school or lineage family and required inputs before making method-specific claims.
limitations: do not silently merge incompatible formulas
ask for missing inputs before precise claims

source_tiers.label: Modern symbolic adaptation
acceptable_evidence: mapping through qi, form, flow, timing, support, leakage, balance, or wuxing
source_tiers.output_posture: Label as modern symbolic adaptation and use it after the domain's real constraints.
limitations: not a substitute for professional review

source_tiers.label: Practical and professional constraint
source_tiers.output_posture: Treat as mandatory before symbolic interpretation.
limitations: feng shui cannot override professional duties
symbolic preference cannot replace evidence or safety

required_checks: avoid saying one text defines all practice

red_lines: do not collapse all schools into one rule
do not invent missing formula steps
do not present scaffold tools as complete lineage engines

required_checks: explain the wuxing or qi mapping
separate real-world constraints from symbolic reading
red_lines: do not present modern adapters as classical doctrine
do not guarantee outcomes from symbolic fit
do not use feng shui as the deciding authority
do not provide medical legal financial engineering tax or safety advice

required_labels: external trusted chart or almanac required
required_checks: ask for externally supplied chart attributes if needed
use available scaffolds only as limited context
red_lines: do not present ganzhi year, moon phase, solar terms, or flying-star scaffold as full bazi qimen liuren zi wei or tong shu work

required_checks: ask for location or timezone when precision matters
state whether using moon phase, solar terms, annual cautions, or almanac attributes
red_lines: do not treat one timing marker as a complete lucky-date formula

citation_rules: Use short source labels such as classical background, school-specific method, lineage-dependent, modern symbolic adaptation, and practical constraint.
Prefer summaries over quotations, and avoid long copyrighted translation excerpts.
When adding a new formula, document the required inputs, lineage assumptions, and limitations before making it operational.
When adding a modern adapter, explain the mapping and keep native-domain constraints first.
When a claim may have changed, such as current standards, laws, financial context, or software integration behavior, verify it before presenting it as current.
red_lines: Do not present modern symbolic adapters as classical doctrine.
Do not claim guaranteed wealth, health, marriage, pregnancy, career, legal, safety, or market outcomes.
Do not silently mix incompatible feng shui schools or lineage formulas.
Do not invent bazi, zi wei, qimen, liuren, tong shu, almanac, or full natal-chart calculations.
Do not let symbolic interpretation override safety, law, medicine, finance, engineering, accessibility, or user agency.
